title: rubus fuscus subsp. quincyi
intro: Rubus fuscus subsp. quincyi, also known as Quince-leaved Blackberry, is a subspecies of bramble native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant's roots, leaves, and fruits are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, offering potential health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, enhanced cognitive function, and reduced risk of certain cancers. Rubus fuscus subsp. quincyi can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has been used in traditional herbal medicine to support overall well-being and promote a healthy digestive system.
